name: Reese's Peanut Butter Cake
recipeInstructions:
In a large mixing bowl, cream together the butter and peanut butter until light and fluffy. Add the brown sugar and mix to blend. Add the eggs one at a time, mixing well after each addition.
In a small bowl, combine the flour, baking powder, and salt. Alternately add the flour mixture and milk to the butter and peanut butter mixture, beginning and ending with the flour mixture. Stir in the vanilla.
Pour the batter into two well-greased and floured 9-inch cake pans. Bake in a preheated 350°F oven until a toothpick inserted into the center of the cakes comes out clean, about 45 minutes. Cool on wire racks to room temperature before frosting.
To prepare the peanut butter filling, cream all ingredients together until light and fluffy.
For the chocolate glaze, place water and butter in a small saucepan and bring to a boil. Add cocoa, sugar, and vanilla, then mix until smooth.
Assemble the cake by spreading half of the peanut butter filling over the tops of each cake using a metal spatula dipped in hot water. Spread the glaze over the cake while it is warm. Once the glaze has cooled, it will thicken.
This recipe yields two 9-inch cakes or one stacked layer cake. If making a stacked layer cake, place the peanut butter filling between the layers and on top of the cake before topping with the chocolate glaze.
